theme_summary: Audit commander launch path, metaswarm gating, branch/task claim flow, and no silent downgrade behavior.
objective: Final pre-build QA review of metaswarm and SwarmMesh readiness using the synced launcher and fixed Agent Pulse. Verify commander launch, premium model policy, collaboration bus creation, sub-agent telemetry, Agent Pulse live counts, stop/recovery behavior, and implementation gaps.
blockers: All 5 workers launched and completed, but the task tool in this environment returned synchronous results and did not expose background agent ids, so launch_started events could not include returned background agent ids as requested.
consensus_notes: Commander launch profile, SS-250 sizing, leaf-node restrictions, and no-silent-downgrade expectations are consistently documented across launcher metadata, commander contract, skill text, and docs.
Premium model policy and banned child-model lists are clearly defined in commander/launcher artifacts, but several checks rely on prompts/metadata rather than hard runtime validation.
Telemetry expectations are strong: swarm-state.json and child-agents.jsonl are treated as required launch proof and live-count evidence.
conflict_notes: Launcher default model remains claude-haiku-4.5 outside explicit overrides, while metaswarm contracts require premium child models; enforcement is contextual rather than universal.
Documentation mentions premium child-model overrides but does not enumerate the premium pool or banned models, creating a docs-to-contract gap.
bin/stampede.sh reports a 1600-worker cap message even though 5 commanders × 250 workers implies 1250 leaf workers.

key_findings.summary: No-silent-downgrade and partial/failure expectations are explicit, but some enforcement still depends on prompts/contracts rather than launcher-side verification.
Telemetry and launch proof are a strong point: swarm-state.json and child-agents.jsonl are consistently required for real-time tracking.
Documentation is directionally aligned on metaswarm behavior and partial-status requirements, but omits banned-model details and detailed stop/recovery guidance.

findings: Metaswarm is explicitly gated by --metaswarm and seeds commander-specific progress/telemetry files.
Launcher default model is still claude-haiku-4.5 unless overridden, so premium child policy is not globally enforced by launcher defaults.
Metaswarm cap error text appears numerically inconsistent with 5×250 leaf-worker math.
Commander contract explicitly defines premium pool, banned models, leaf-node lock, launch proof schema, and partial/failed status rules.
Collaboration bus creation is not covered in the commander contract.
Recovery guidance is thin beyond retry_budget and status reporting.
SKILL.md clearly phrases metaswarm as explicit opt-in and requires partial/failed reporting for under-launch.
Skill text does not itself spell out the premium pool or banned-model list.
Teardown/resume grammar exists, but collaboration-bus language is absent.
README and AGENTS align on nested progress, partial-status expectations, and branch/task isolation at a high level.
Docs do not enumerate banned models or the premium model pool.
Stop/teardown/recovery details remain sparse in docs.
Banned/cheap models are rejected at metaswarm launch time rather than silently downgraded.
Smoke tests cover premium/banned model metadata, helping catch downgrade regressions.
Partial status for incomplete launches is defined, but runtime proof of actual launched child models is still telemetry-driven.
